decisions.decision: Milestone v1.4 'Calendar Visual Clarity' picks up DISPLAY-01 and DISPLAY-02 from the v1.3-deferred v2 candidates list
decisions.rationale: User explicitly chose to complete both in this milestone rather than deferring DISPLAY-02 further (its original deferral condition was 'only if operators report confusion' — user decided to build it now anyway since the surrounding color work is already happening)

decisions.decision: Color is keyed on proposal, not telescope
decisions.rationale: User: an LCO proposal can span 2m0/1m0/0m4 telescopes/instruments and should render as one consistent color for the shared science theme, regardless of which telescope a block lands on; non-LCO proposals already bind ~1:1 to a telescope so proposal-keying subsumes that case too

decisions.decision: Status visual treatment (opacity/border vs. striping) deferred to a /gsd:sketch session during phase planning, not decided now
decisions.rationale: User explicitly wants a visual comparison before committing — this is a design call, not just engineering; research (FEATURES.md) pre-vetted options (border-style favored, accessibility-checked) as sketch-session input

decisions.decision: Phase order: DISPLAY-02 (sidecar model + migration) before DISPLAY-01 (template-only color/status)
decisions.rationale: ARCHITECTURE.md research: DISPLAY-02 is solsys_code's first-ever migration/cross-app model extension — the riskier, more novel piece — isolating it first keeps DISPLAY-01's purely-additive template work from being entangled with schema risk

decisions.decision: N+1 query risk on DISPLAY-02's sidecar reverse-accessor: accept as-is this milestone, no batching template tag
decisions.rationale: User confirmed current calendar-event volume doesn't justify the extra scope; documented as a known, bounded cost in research, not silently ignored

decisions.decision: DISPLAY-01 proposal coloring applies to both all-day and timed calendar-event branches
decisions.rationale: User confirmed scope should match the goal (proposal identity visible regardless of render branch) rather than match today's narrower all-day-only behavior

decisions.decision: Empty-proposal events (classical schedule, load_telescope_runs never sets proposal) get a dedicated neutral palette slot
decisions.rationale: User confirmed — avoids an empty string's hash coincidentally colliding with a real proposal's color

blocking_constraints.name: Parallel research agents can silently fail without erroring
blocking_constraints.description: 2 of 4 parallel gsd-project-researcher agents (Architecture, Pitfalls) hit a session/usage limit mid-run and returned a 'completed' task-notification with near-zero token usage, but had NOT actually written their output file — the stale file from the PRIOR milestone's research was left in place with no error surfaced. Only caught by checking file mtimes after the notification, not by trusting the notification text alone.
blocking_constraints.mitigation: After spawning parallel research/synthesis agents, always verify output file mtimes are fresh (today, not a stale prior date) before treating the step as done — do not rely solely on the 'completed' task-notification status. If stale, re-spawn that specific agent with an explicit note that a prior attempt failed and left stale content, telling it to overwrite.

blocking_constraints.name: gsd-research-synthesizer's Write tool can be hard-blocked
blocking_constraints.description: The synthesizer subagent's Write tool call failed with 'Subagents should return findings as text, not write report files' even though writing SUMMARY.md to disk is its designated job per its own prompt. It worked around this via a Bash heredoc-to-scratchpad-then-cp pattern, but took ~9 minutes and the orchestrator ended up writing the file directly anyway (with content read from the agent's transcript) rather than waiting further.
blocking_constraints.mitigation: If a synthesizer/writer-role subagent stalls after a Write-tool rejection, it's faster for the orchestrator to extract the already-produced content from the agent's own transcript/result and write it directly than to wait for the agent's Bash-workaround to land.
